before_code:
# Old: just delete or git mv the file
git mv Mathlib/Foo.lean Mathlib/Foo/Bar.lean
# Then update Mathlib.lean and break downstream callers
after_code:
# CHECK FIRST: did the file exist on master?
#   git ls-tree master -- <path>
# (`--diff-filter=A` is unreliable across rebases — use ls-tree, which
# directly inspects master.)
# If the file is NOT on master (added in the same PR), no deprecation is
# needed — just put it in the right place from the start (move + update
# imports). If it IS on master, downstream code may import it, so leave a
# deprecation shim.
#
# Workflow:
#
# Step 1: git mv Foo.lean Foo/Bar.lean (or copy content to new path)
# Step 2: At the OLD path, write a deprecation shim:
#
#   /- copyright header -/
#   module -- shake: keep-all
#
#   public import Mathlib.<NewLocation>
#   public import Mathlib.Tactic.Linter.DeprecatedModule
#
#   deprecated_module "Use `Mathlib.<NewLocation>` instead."
#     (since := "yyyy-mm-dd")
#
# Step 3: Update Mathlib.lean to register BOTH the old (shim) path and the
#         new path, alphabetically sorted.
# Step 4: Update internal callers to use the new path. The shim keeps
#         downstream users (and this PR's transitional commits) working
#         while emitting a deprecation warning.
description: When moving a file: first check whether the old path exists on master (`git ls-tree master -- <path>`; do NOT trust `--diff-filter=A` — it is unreliable across rebases). If the file is NOT on master, it was added in this PR; just move it, no deprecation needed. If it IS on master, downstream code may import it, so leave a deprecation shim: a `module -- shake: keep-all` stub that `public import`s the new location and adds `deprecated_module "<message>" (since := "yyyy-mm-dd")`. Both old and new paths get listed in `Mathlib.lean`. Update internal callers to import the new path so only downstream code triggers the deprecation warning.

before_code:
-- Renamed `ModularFormClass.qExpansion_smul` to `ModularForm.qExpansion_smul`,
-- old name is just gone
after_code:
-- After renaming, in the OLD namespace add:
namespace ModularFormClass

@[deprecated (since := "yyyy-mm-dd")]
protected alias qExpansion_smul := ModularForm.qExpansion_smul

-- ... etc for every renamed declaration

end ModularFormClass
description: When renaming declarations across namespaces (e.g. `Foo.bar` → `FooClass.bar`, or vice versa), don't just delete the old name — add `@[deprecated (since := "yyyy-mm-dd")] protected alias oldName := NewNamespace.newName` at the OLD namespace. `protected` keeps the old qualified-name only access. This applies to PRs that move/rename anything that downstream code might import.
